The quoting is tangled. The Python code uses single quotes (`{'name': ...}`) inside a double-quoted shell string, and the closing `"` on line 71 is ambiguous. Let me fix it properly.




Now the Python code is wrapped in single quotes (no shell expansion inside), and the Python strings use double quotes. The shell variables are passed as separate arguments outside the quotes. Run it again:

```bash
cd /opt/data/weather-app && bash create-and-push.sh
```
